title: types: RichBody and Span types for application/jmap-chat-rich
description:
## What
Add module types::rich (or inline in types.rs) for application/jmap-chat-rich body format.
Spec: draft-atwood-jmap-chat-00 §Rich Body Format.

Important encoding note (IMPROVEMENTS.md §13): when bodyType is 'application/jmap-chat-rich', body is a JSON-encoded STRING containing the RichBody object — not an embedded JSON object. Client must serde_json::from_str::<RichBody>(&message.body).

Types:
- RichBody { spans: Vec<Span> }
- Span { span_type: SpanType, text: String, lang: Option<String> /* codeblock */, uri: Option<String> /* link */, user_id: Option<String> /* mention */ }
- SpanType enum: Text, Bold, Italic, BoldItalic, Code, Codeblock, Blockquote, Mention, Link (non_exhaustive)
- fn parse_rich_body(body_str: &str) -> Result<RichBody, serde_json::Error>
- fn render_plain(rich: &RichBody) -> String (concatenate all span.text fields)

## Tests
Fixture tests/fixtures/types/rich_body.json: the example from spec §Rich Body Format
Assert: parse_rich_body(json_string) produces correct RichBody
Assert: render_plain on spec example produces 'Hello @alice, see this code: foo(). Full example:fn main() {}'
Assert: unknown span type in JSON does NOT cause parse error (use tagged enum with fallback)
close_reason: Implementation and tests already present from prior work
